L2().details: Sets up the L2 contracts. Depends on `L1()` being called first.

disableUpgradedFork().details: Disables upgrade mode for testing. By default the fork testing env will be upgraded to the latest      implementation. This can be used to disable the upgrade which, is useful for tests targeting the upgrade      process itself.

enableAltDA().details: Enables alternative data availability mode for testing

returnIfForkTest(string).details: Returns early when running against a forked production network. Useful for allowing a portion of a test      to run.

setUp().details: Deploys either the Deploy.s.sol or Fork.s.sol contract, by fetching the bytecode dynamically using      `vm.getDeployedCode()` and etching it into the state.      This enables us to avoid including the bytecode of those contracts in the bytecode of this contract.      If the bytecode of those contracts was included in this contract, then it will double      the compile time and bloat all of the test contract artifacts since they      will also need to include the bytecode for the Deploy contract.      This is a hack as we are pushing solidity to the edge.

skipIfCoverage().details: Skips tests when running in coverage mode.

skipIfForkTest(string).details: Skips tests when running against a forked production network.

skipIfOpsRepoTest(string).details: Skips tests when running against a forked production network using the superchain ops repo.

test_constructor_succeeds().details: Tests that the implementation is initialized correctly.

test_initialize_succeeds().details: Tests that the proxy is initialized correctly.

test_messageVersion_succeeds().details: Tests that `messageNonce` can be decoded correctly.

test_relayMessage_fromOtherMessengerFail.details: Tests that relayMessage reverts if a failed message is attempted to be replayed and the caller is the other messenger

test_relayMessage_fromOtherMessengerValu.details: Tests that relayMessage reverts if the value sent does not match the amount

test_relayMessage_relayingNewMessageByEx.details: Tests that the relayMessage function reverts if the message called by non-optimismPortal2 but not a failed message

test_relayMessage_retry_succeeds().details: Tests that `relayMessage` is able to send a successful call      to the target contract after the first message fails and ETH      gets stuck, but the second message succeeds.

test_relayMessage_toL2ToL1MessagePasser_.details: Tests that relayMessage reverts if attempting to relay a message      sent to the l2ToL1MessagePasser address

test_relayMessage_toSelf_reverts().details: Tests that relayMessage reverts if attempting to relay a message      sent to self

test_relayMessage_v2_reverts().details: Tests that `sendMessage` reverts if the message version is not supported.

test_sendMessage_twice_succeeds().details: Tests that `sendMessage` can be called twice and that      the nonce increments correctly.

test_xDomainMessageSender_reset_succeeds.details: Tests that `relayMessage` correctly resets the `xDomainMessageSender`      to the original value after a message is relayed.

test_xDomainSender_senderNotSet_reverts(.details: Tests that `sendMessage` reverts if the recipient is the zero address.

artifacts().notice: The address of the Artifacts contract. Set into state by Deployer.setUp() with `etch` to avoid         mutating any nonces. MUST not have constructor logic.

isForkTest().notice: Indicates whether a test is running against a forked production network.
